content: Large-graph WebGL viz: sigma.js renders thousands-to-millions of nodes/edges via WebGL with interactive pan/zoom/hover/click. Cosmograph uses GPU force-directed layout for million-node graphs with straight-line edges (no edge bundling). Edge bundling and tile-pyramid routing (GraphMaps, Cornac) reduce clutter but need heavy precompute / multi-server backends — too expensive for a live in-browser tool. Density maps (smoothed 2D histograms of edge occurrence) are a cheaper clutter-reduction alternative. Directionality cues and bundling are differentiators, not table stakes.
